sentences.sentence: More than two hundred years ago, Russian explorers and fur hunters landed on the Aleutian Islands, a volcanic archipelago in the North Pacific, and learned of a land mass that lay farther to the north.
sentences.translation: 两百多年前，俄罗斯探险家和毛皮猎人登上阿留申群岛——北太平洋上的一片火山群岛，并得知更北方还有一片陆地。

structure.text: More than two hundred years ago
a volcanic archipelago in the North Pacific
and learned of a land mass
that lay farther to the north

difficult_points: a volcanic archipelago... 是 Aleutian Islands 的同位语，补充其地理性质和位置。
that 引导定语从句修饰 a land mass，lay 在这里表示“位于”，不是 lie 的过去式“说谎”。

sentences.sentence: The islands' native inhabitants called this land mass Aleyska, the 'Great Land'; today, we know it as Alaska.
sentences.translation: 岛上的原住民把这片陆地称为 Aleyska，意为“伟大的土地”；如今，我们称它为阿拉斯加。
difficult_points: call A B 是“把 A 称为 B”的复合宾语结构，Aleyska 作宾语补足语。
分号连接今昔两个完整分句；it 回指 this land mass。

sentences.sentence: The forty-ninth state to join the United States of America (in 1959), Alaska is fully one-fifth the size of the mainland 48 states combined.
sentences.translation: 阿拉斯加于1959年成为美国第49个州，其面积足有美国本土48州总面积的五分之一。

structure.text: The forty-ninth state to join the United States of America (in 1959)
fully one-fifth the size of the mainland 48 states combined

difficult_points: 句首名词短语是 Alaska 的同位语，to join... 后置说明“第49个加入美国的州”。
one-fifth the size of... 是倍数比较表达；combined 是过去分词，表示48个州合在一起计算。

sentences.sentence: It shares, with Canada, the second longest river system in North America and has over half the coastline of the United States.
sentences.translation: 它与加拿大共同拥有北美第二长的河流系统，同时拥有美国一半以上的海岸线。

structure.text: the second longest river system in North America
over half the coastline of the United States

difficult_points: with Canada 被逗号隔开，说明河流系统由阿拉斯加与加拿大共享，并不割裂 shares 与宾语。
shares 和 has 是由 and 连接的两个并列谓语，共用主语 It。

sentences.sentence: The rivers feed into the Bering Sea and Gulf of Alaska - cold, nutrient-rich waters which support tens of millions of seabirds, and over 400 species of fish, shellfish, crustaceans, and molluscs.
sentences.translation: 这些河流汇入白令海和阿拉斯加湾——这两片寒冷且营养丰富的水域养育着数千万只海鸟，以及400多种鱼类、贝类、甲壳类和软体动物。

structure.text: the Bering Sea and Gulf of Alaska
which support tens of millions of seabirds, and over 400 species of fish, shellfish, crustaceans, and molluscs

difficult_points: 破折号后的 cold, nutrient-rich waters 是前面两片海域的同位说明。
which 指代 waters，并在定语从句中作主语；support 在此意为“维持……生存、养育”。

sentences.sentence: Taking advantage of this rich bounty, Alaska's commercial fisheries have developed into some of the largest in the world.
sentences.translation: 凭借如此丰富的资源，阿拉斯加的商业渔业已经发展为世界上规模最大的渔业之一。

structure.text: Taking advantage of this rich bounty
some of the largest in the world

difficult_points: Taking advantage of... 是现在分词短语作方式或原因状语，逻辑主语是 Alaska's commercial fisheries。
some of the largest 后省略了 fisheries，避免与主语重复。

sentences.sentence: According to the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G), Alaska's commercial fisheries landed hundreds of thousands of tonnes of shellfish and herring, and well over a million tonnes of groundfish (cod, sole, perch and pollock) in 2000.
sentences.translation: 据阿拉斯加州渔猎部（ADF&G）统计，2000年阿拉斯加商业渔业捕捞了数十万吨贝类和鲱鱼，以及远超一百万吨的底层鱼类（鳕鱼、鳎鱼、鲈鱼和狭鳕）。

structure.text: According to the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G)
hundreds of thousands of tonnes of shellfish and herring, and well over a million tonnes of groundfish (cod, sole, perch and pollock)

difficult_points: landed 在渔业语境中表示“捕捞并卸岸”，不是普通的“着陆”。
and 连接两个数量宾语；括号内列举 groundfish 的具体种类。

sentences.sentence: The true cultural heart and soul of Alaska's fisheries, however, is salmon.
sentences.translation: 然而，真正构成阿拉斯加渔业文化核心与灵魂的是鲑鱼。

structure.text: The true cultural heart and soul of Alaska's fisheries

difficult_points: however 插在主语与系动词之间，表示与前文丰富渔获形成转折。
heart and soul 是比喻性并列名词，强调鲑鱼在文化上的核心地位。

sentences.sentence: 'Salmon,' notes writer Susan Ewing in The Great Alaska Nature Factbook, 'pump through Alaska like blood through a heart, bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people.'
sentences.translation: 作家苏珊·尤因在《阿拉斯加自然百科全书》中写道：“鲑鱼如同血液流经心脏一般穿行于阿拉斯加，为土地、动物和人类循环不断地带来滋养。”

structure.text: notes writer Susan Ewing in The Great Alaska Nature Factbook
bringing rhythmic, circulating nourishment to land, animals and people

difficult_points: notes writer Susan Ewing 是插入引语内部的报道语，主句 Salmon pump... 被它隔开。
bringing... 是现在分词短语，表示鲑鱼穿行所带来的伴随结果，逻辑主语为 Salmon。

sentences.sentence: The 'predictable abundance of salmon allowed some native cultures to flourish,' and 'dying spawners* feed bears, eagles, other animals, and ultimately the soil itself.'
sentences.translation: “鲑鱼数量稳定而丰富，使一些原住民文化得以繁荣”，而且“濒死的产卵鱼会滋养熊、鹰、其他动物，最终还会滋养土壤本身”。

structure.text: The 'predictable abundance of salmon allowed some native cultures to flourish,'
allowed some native cultures to flourish
and 'dying spawners* feed bears, eagles, other animals, and ultimately the soil itself.'
feed bears, eagles, other animals, and ultimately the soil itself

difficult_points: allow + 宾语 + to do 表示“使某人或事物能够做……”，to flourish 是宾语补足语。
dying spawners 指产卵后濒死的鲑鱼；feed 在此既含“喂养动物”，也含“为土壤提供养分”。

sentences.sentence: All five species of Pacific salmon - chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback - spawn** in Alaskan waters, and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there.
sentences.translation: 太平洋鲑鱼的五个种类——奇努克鲑（帝王鲑）、狗鲑、银鲑、红鲑和粉红鲑——都在阿拉斯加水域产卵；北美商业捕捞的太平洋鲑鱼中，有90%产自那里。

structure.text: All five species of Pacific salmon
chinook, or king; chum, or dog; coho, or silver; sockeye, or red; and pink, or humpback
and 90% of all Pacific salmon commercially caught in North America are produced there

difficult_points: 两个破折号之间列出五种鲑鱼及其别称，是主语 five species 的同位说明。
commercially caught in North America 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 all Pacific salmon；there 指 Alaska。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, if Alaska was an independent nation, it would be the largest producer of wild salmon in the world.
sentences.translation: 事实上，如果阿拉斯加是一个独立国家，它将会是世界上最大的野生鲑鱼产地。

structure.text: if Alaska was an independent nation
the largest producer of wild salmon in the world

difficult_points: if 从句提出与现实不符的假设：阿拉斯加实际上并非独立国家。
would be 与条件从句呼应，说明假设成立时的结果；it 指 Alaska。

sentences.sentence: During 2000, commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska exceeded 320,000 tonnes, with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million.
sentences.translation: 2000年，阿拉斯加太平洋鲑鱼的商业捕捞量超过32万吨，其离船交易价值逾2.6亿美元。

structure.text: commercial catches of Pacific salmon in Alaska
with an ex-vessel value of over $US260 million

difficult_points: catches 在这里是可数名词，指商业捕捞所得的总量。
with 引出伴随数据；ex-vessel value 指渔获离开渔船时的初次销售价值。

sentences.sentence: Catches have not always been so healthy.
sentences.translation: 渔获量并非一直都如此可观。
difficult_points: 现在完成时概括从过去到现在的历史情况，not always 表示“并非一直”。
healthy 修饰 catches 时不是“健康的”，而是“数量充足、状况良好”。

sentences.sentence: Between 1940 and 1959, overfishing led to crashes in salmon populations so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area.
sentences.translation: 1940年至1959年间，过度捕捞导致鲑鱼种群数量暴跌，情况严重到阿拉斯加于1953年被宣布为联邦灾区。

structure.text: so severe that in 1953 Alaska was declared a federal disaster area

difficult_points: lead to 后接名词 crashes，表示过度捕捞造成种群数量骤降。
so severe that... 是结果结构，severe 描述 crashes 的严重程度，that 从句给出后果。

sentences.sentence: With the onset of statehood, however, the State of Alaska took over management of its own fisheries, guided by a state constitution which mandates that Alaska's natural resources be managed on a sustainable basis.
sentences.translation: 然而，随着阿拉斯加正式建州，州政府接管了本州渔业的管理工作；州宪法为此提供指导，并规定阿拉斯加的自然资源必须以可持续方式管理。

structure.text: which mandates that Alaska's natural resources be managed on a sustainable basis
that Alaska's natural resources be managed on a sustainable basis

difficult_points: guided by... 是过去分词短语，说明州政府接管管理工作时所遵循的依据。
which 指代 a state constitution；mandates 后的 that 从句使用 be managed 这一虚拟形式，表达法规要求。

sentences.sentence: At that time, statewide harvests totalled around 25 million salmon.
sentences.translation: 当时，全州的鲑鱼捕捞量总计约为2500万条。
difficult_points: harvests 在渔业语境中指“捕捞量”，而非农作物收成。
totalled 是及物动词，直接接数量，表示“总计达到”。

sentences.sentence: Over the next few decades average catches steadily increased as a result of this policy of sustainable management, until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish.
sentences.translation: 在随后的几十年里，由于实行这一可持续管理政策，平均捕捞量稳步上升；到20世纪90年代，年捕捞量已远超1亿条，并有数次超过2亿条。

structure.text: as a result of this policy of sustainable management
until, during the 1990s, annual harvests were well in excess of 100 million, and on several occasions over 200 million fish

difficult_points: until 引出的从句说明增长最终达到的阶段，而 during the 1990s 是插入其中的时间状语。
and on several occasions over 200 million fish 省略了 annual harvests were，需从前半句补全理解。

sentences.sentence: The primary reason for such increases is what is known as 'In-Season Abundance-Based Management'.
sentences.translation: 这种增长的首要原因，是所谓的“渔季内按资源丰度管理”机制。

structure.text: The primary reason for such increases
what is known as 'In-Season Abundance-Based Management'

difficult_points: what is known as... 是融合关系结构，整体作表语，可理解为“被称为……的做法”。
such increases 回指上一段捕捞量从约2500万增长至上亿的现象。

sentences.sentence: There are biologists throughout the state constantly monitoring adult fish as they show up to spawn.
sentences.translation: 全州各地都有生物学家持续监测前来产卵的成年鱼。

structure.text: as they show up to spawn

difficult_points: monitoring... 是现在分词短语，说明这些 biologists 持续进行的工作。
as 引导时间从句；they 指 adult fish，to spawn 表示鱼出现的目的。

sentences.sentence: The biologists sit in streamside counting towers, study sonar, watch from aeroplanes, and talk to fishermen.
sentences.translation: 生物学家们坐在溪边的计数塔中，研究声呐数据，从飞机上观察，并与渔民交谈。
difficult_points: sit、study、watch 和 talk 是四个并列谓语，共用主语 The biologists。
streamside 是复合形容词修饰 counting towers，表示计数塔位于溪流旁。

sentences.sentence: The salmon season in Alaska is not pre-set.
sentences.translation: 阿拉斯加的鲑鱼捕捞季并非预先固定。
difficult_points: pre-set 是过去分词形容词，表示捕捞季的时间不是事先固定好的。
not 否定的是 pre-set，而不是说阿拉斯加不存在鲑鱼季。

sentences.sentence: The fishermen know the approximate time of year when they will be allowed to fish, but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing.
sentences.translation: 渔民知道一年中大致何时会获准捕鱼，但在任何一天，特定地区的一名或多名野外生物学家都可以叫停捕捞。

structure.text: when they will be allowed to fish
but on any given day, one or more field biologists in a particular area can put a halt to fishing

difficult_points: when 引导定语从句修饰 time of year，will be allowed 是将来时的被动语态。
but 后的完整分句构成转折；put a halt to 中 to 是介词，后接动名词 fishing。

sentences.sentence: Even sport fishing can be brought to a halt.
sentences.translation: 甚至休闲垂钓也可能被叫停。

structure.text: can be brought to a halt

difficult_points: Even 强调管理范围不仅限于商业捕捞，还包括休闲垂钓。
can be brought 是情态动词加被动语态，表示管理者有权让它停止。

sentences.sentence: It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper, even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered.
sentences.translation: 正是这种管理机制，使阿拉斯加的鲑鱼种群——进而也使阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业——得以繁荣；与此同时，美国其他地区的鲑鱼种群却日益被视为受威胁甚至濒危。

structure.text: It is this management mechanism that has allowed Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries - to prosper
Alaska salmon stocks - and, accordingly, Alaska salmon fisheries -
even as salmon populations in the rest of the United States are increasingly considered threatened or even endangered

difficult_points: It is ... that ... 是强调句，突出真正促成繁荣的是 this management mechanism。
allow + 宾语 + to prosper 中，破折号插入并列宾语 Alaska salmon fisheries；accordingly 表示后者由前者繁荣而相应受益。
even as 引出对比背景；are considered 后接 threatened or endangered 作主语补足语。

sentences.sentence: In 1999, the Marine Stewardship Council (MSC)*** commissioned a review of the Alaska salmon fishery.
sentences.translation: 1999年，海洋管理委员会（MSC）委托开展了一项对阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业的审查。

structure.text: a review of the Alaska salmon fishery

difficult_points: commission 在这里是动词，表示正式委托他人开展审查。
of the Alaska salmon fishery 后置修饰 review，说明审查对象。

sentences.sentence: The Council, which was founded in 1996, certifies fisheries that meet high environmental standards, enabling them to use a label that recognises their environmental responsibility.
sentences.translation: 该委员会成立于1996年，负责认证符合高环境标准的渔业，使其能够使用一种认可其环境责任的标志。

structure.text: enabling them to use a label

difficult_points: which was founded... 是非限制性定语从句，补充 Council 的成立时间；第二个 that 从句修饰 fisheries。
enabling... 是现在分词结果状语，them 指通过认证的 fisheries；最后一个 that 修饰 a label。

sentences.sentence: The MSC has established a set of criteria by which commercial fisheries can be judged.
sentences.translation: MSC已经建立了一套评判商业渔业的标准。

structure.text: by which commercial fisheries can be judged

difficult_points: by which 是“介词 + 关系代词”结构，修饰 criteria，相当于 according to which。
commercial fisheries can be judged 是被动语态，表示这些渔业依据标准接受评判。

sentences.sentence: Recognising the potential benefits of being identified as environmentally responsible, fisheries approach the Council requesting to undergo the certification process.
sentences.translation: 渔业机构认识到被认定为对环境负责可能带来的益处，便向委员会提出申请，要求接受认证程序。

structure.text: Recognising the potential benefits of being identified as environmentally responsible
requesting to undergo the certification process

difficult_points: Recognising... 是原因性的现在分词状语，逻辑主语 fisheries 在此指渔业机构或经营者。
being identified 是动名词的被动式，作 of 的宾语；requesting... 表示向委员会接洽时提出的请求。

sentences.sentence: The MSC then appoints a certification committee, composed of a panel of fisheries experts, which gathers information and opinions from fishermen, biologists, government officials, industry representatives, non-governmental organisations and others.
sentences.translation: 随后，MSC任命一个由渔业专家小组组成的认证委员会；该委员会从渔民、生物学家、政府官员、行业代表、非政府组织及其他相关方那里收集信息和意见。

structure.text: composed of a panel of fisheries experts
which gathers information and opinions from fishermen, biologists, government officials, industry representatives, non-governmental organisations and others

difficult_points: composed of... 是过去分词短语，修饰 certification committee，表示委员会的人员构成。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代 committee，而不是紧邻的 experts。

sentences.sentence: Some observers thought the Alaska salmon fisheries would not have any chance of certification when, in the months leading up to MSC's final decision, salmon runs throughout western Alaska completely collapsed.
sentences.translation: 一些观察人士原以为，阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业将毫无获得认证的机会，因为在MSC作出最终决定前的几个月里，阿拉斯加西部各地的鲑鱼洄游量彻底崩溃。

structure.text: the Alaska salmon fisheries would not have any chance of certification
when, in the months leading up to MSC's final decision, salmon runs throughout western Alaska completely collapsed
leading up to MSC's final decision

difficult_points: thought 后接省略 that 的宾语从句，would 表示从当时看未来获得认证的可能。
when 从句说明产生这一判断时发生的情况；leading up to... 修饰 months，意为“临近最终决定的”。
salmon runs 指鲑鱼成群洄游及其数量，不是“鲑鱼奔跑”。

sentences.sentence: In the Yukon and Kuskokwim rivers, chinook and chum runs were probably the poorest since statehood; subsistence communities throughout the region, who normally have priority over commercial fishing, were devastated.
sentences.translation: 在育空河和库斯科奎姆河，奇努克鲑和狗鲑的洄游情况可能是建州以来最差的；整个地区依靠渔获维生的社区遭受重创，而这些社区通常享有高于商业捕捞的优先权。

structure.text: In the Yukon and Kuskokwim rivers
who normally have priority over commercial fishing

difficult_points: 分号连接两个紧密相关的完整分句：洄游量暴跌，依赖鲑鱼生存的社区因此受创。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充 subsistence communities 通常拥有的捕捞优先权。

sentences.sentence: The crisis was completely unexpected, but researchers believe it had nothing to do with impacts of fisheries.
sentences.translation: 这场危机完全出乎意料，但研究人员认为它与渔业活动的影响毫无关系。

structure.text: but researchers believe it had nothing to do with impacts of fisheries
it had nothing to do with impacts of fisheries

difficult_points: but 连接两个完整分句，先说明危机意外，再排除渔业活动这一原因。
believe 后省略 that；it 指 The crisis，have nothing to do with 表示“与……无关”。

sentences.sentence: Rather, they contend, it was almost certainly the result of climatic shifts, prompted in part by cumulative effects of the el niño / la niña phenomenon on Pacific Ocean temperatures, culminating in a harsh winter in which huge numbers of salmon eggs were frozen.
sentences.translation: 相反，研究人员认为，这场危机几乎可以肯定是气候变化造成的；这种变化部分由厄尔尼诺/拉尼娜现象对太平洋水温的累积影响所引发，最终导致一个严酷的冬季，大量鲑鱼卵在其中被冻死。

structure.text: almost certainly the result of climatic shifts
prompted in part by cumulative effects of the el niño / la niña phenomenon on Pacific Ocean temperatures
in which huge numbers of salmon eggs were frozen

difficult_points: they contend 是插入的报道语，it 回指上一句的 crisis；主干是 it was ... the result of climatic shifts。
prompted... 是过去分词短语，修饰 climatic shifts 并说明诱因；culminating... 表示这些变化最终导致的结果。
in which 修饰 a harsh winter，表示大量鱼卵在那个冬季被冻住或冻死。

sentences.sentence: It could have meant the end as far as the certification process was concerned.
sentences.translation: 就认证程序而言，这本可能意味着整个过程就此终结。

structure.text: as far as the certification process was concerned

difficult_points: could have meant 是情态动词完成式，表示当时存在但最终未发生的可能后果。
as far as ... was concerned 限定判断范围，意为“就认证程序而言”。

sentences.sentence: However, the state reacted quickly, closing down all fisheries, even those necessary for subsistence purposes.
sentences.translation: 然而，州政府迅速作出反应，关闭了所有渔业活动，甚至包括维持生计所必需的捕捞。

structure.text: even those necessary for subsistence purposes

difficult_points: closing down... 是现在分词短语，说明州政府迅速反应所采取的具体行动。
those 代替 fisheries；necessary for subsistence purposes 是形容词短语，后置修饰 those。

sentences.sentence: In September 2000, MSC announced that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification.
sentences.translation: 2000年9月，MSC宣布阿拉斯加鲑鱼渔业符合认证资格。

structure.text: that the Alaska salmon fisheries qualified for certification

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明 MSC 宣布的具体内容。
qualify for 表示达到条件而有资格获得某项认证。

sentences.sentence: Seven companies producing Alaska salmon were immediately granted permission to display the MSC logo on their products.
sentences.translation: 七家生产阿拉斯加鲑鱼产品的公司立即获准在产品上展示MSC标志。

structure.text: to display the MSC logo on their products

difficult_points: producing... 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 Seven companies。
were granted permission 是双宾语结构 grant sb permission 的被动形式；to display... 说明获准做什么。

sentences.sentence: Certification is for an initial period of five years, with an annual review to ensure that the fishery is continuing to meet the required standards.
sentences.translation: 认证的初始有效期为五年，并每年进行一次审查，以确保该渔业持续达到规定标准。

structure.text: for an initial period of five years
to ensure that the fishery is continuing to meet the required standards
that the fishery is continuing to meet the required standards

difficult_points: for an initial period of five years 说明认证最初的有效期限。
with 引出配套安排；to ensure... 表示年度审查的目的，that 从句作 ensure 的宾语。
